page: Microsoft Azure M64-32ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 1792.0 GB of system memory, yielding 56.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage consists of 2097 GB of Premium Storage capable capacity, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core processing capabilities are relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show poor performance. However, multi-core performance is average, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads and writes remains in the middle 50% tier. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, also perform at an average level. LLM inference speeds are average for prompt processing on small to medium models, but weak to poor for text generation and larger models.

Given the massive memory footprint of 1792.0 GB relative to 32 vCPUs, this server offers high resource density for memory-bound tasks, though it presents a lower CPU-to-RAM cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads due to its modest single-core performance. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications with massive caching requirements, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure M64-32ms is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor running at 2.7 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 1792.0 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage. While single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression benchmarks are weak, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth consistently rank in the average tier. Database operations and Redis SET throughput also perform at average levels. With 56.0 GB of memory per vCPU, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw compute speed. It is highly suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64-32ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 1792 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large-scale databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64-32ms is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 1792 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an exceptional RAM-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8280L processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Massive 1792.0 GB of RAM providing 56.0 GB of memory per vCPU
2097 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for large in-memory databases and enterprise caching layers
